name: apple/container
description: Apple's tool for running Linux containers on macOS, each inside its own lightweight VM on Apple silicon. Docker alternative with per-container kernel isolation.

name: chopratejas/headroom
description: Compression layer that shrinks tool outputs, logs and RAG chunks before they reach the model. 60-95% token reduction, deterministic, no extra model call.

name: dmtrKovalenko/fff
description: Rust file-search toolkit for AI agents and Neovim. Sub-10ms queries on 500k files. Resident in-memory index, SIMD matching, frecency ranking.

name: butterbase-ai/butterbase
description: Open-source AI-native BaaS with Postgres, auth, storage, functions. MCP server exposes 35 tools — agent builds backend via tool calls, not dashboard clicks.

name: kenn-io/agentsview
description: Local-first analytics for coding-agent sessions. Reads Claude Code, Codex, 20+ agent session files, indexes to SQLite, tracks token usage and cost. 100x faster than ccusage.

name: mvanhorn/last30days-skill
description: AI agent skill that researches any topic across Reddit, X, YouTube, HN, Polymarket, and the web — then synthesizes a grounded summary.

name: thedotmack/claude-mem
description: Persistent memory layer across agent sessions. Auto-captures tool use and decisions, generates semantic summaries. 80k+ stars.

name: OpenBMB/VoxCPM
description: Tokenizer-free TTS system. 30 languages, voice design from text, real-time streaming. Apache-2.0.

name: jamiepine/voicebox
description: Private, on-device voice toolkit for TTS, cloning and dictation. Free open-source ElevenLabs alternative.

name: Michael-A-Kuykendall/shimmy
description: Pure-Rust inference engine with OpenAI-API-compatible endpoint. Single binary, no Python, no llama.cpp. Vulkan/D3D12/Metal.

name: NVIDIA/SkillSpector
description: Security scanner for AI agent skills. Detect vulnerabilities, malicious patterns, and security risks.

name: Andyyyy64/whichllm
description: Find the local LLM that actually runs and performs best on your hardware. Ranked by real benchmarks, not parameter count.
